western.definition: Instrument detecting specific variables; produces quantified data; replaceable/standardized.

relational.definition: Any system detecting field patterns; biological, technological, or social; specialized by environment, training, and development.
assumptions: Sensor as specialized capability, not just instrument.

hidden_variables.name: Calibration quality
hidden_variables.description: How well the sensor (biological, social, or instrumental) has been tuned to its operating conditions.
hidden_variables.why_western_misses_it: Specification-based validation tests the device, not the deployment.

hidden_variables.name: Relational awareness
hidden_variables.description: The sensor's situatedness in the relationship being measured.
hidden_variables.why_western_misses_it: Standardized instruments are designed to be context-independent.

hidden_variables.name: Multi-modal integration
hidden_variables.description: Cross-checking signals from multiple sensing modes simultaneously.
hidden_variables.why_western_misses_it: Identical-unit redundancy doesn't catch correlated failure modes.

hidden_variables.name: Developmental history
hidden_variables.description: How the sensing capacity was trained, over what timescale, in what conditions.
hidden_variables.why_western_misses_it: Treated as a manufacturing detail rather than a feature of the deployment.

translation_protocol.when_to_translate: When 'sensors' are being scoped to instruments only, and biological or social sensing is being excluded as anecdote.
translation_protocol.how: Ask: 'What modality is best calibrated to this signal in this environment? Is the answer necessarily an instrument?'
